question_text: A mass of 2 kg is hung from a spring with a spring constant of 200 N/m. The spring extends and then comes to rest. Calculate the extension of the spring at equilibrium. (Use g = 10 m/s²)
explanation: First, calculate the weight of the mass: F = m × g = 2 × 10 = 20 N. At equilibrium, the spring force equals the weight, so using F = k × e: 20 = 200 × e, which gives e = 20 ÷ 200 = 0.1 m. The spring extends 10 centimetres to support the hanging mass.
